description: Septic shock with classic triad (fever + hypotension + altered mental status) in a 58-year-old post-catheterization patient.

patient.name: Marcus J., 58M
patient.chief_complaint: Fever, rigors, confusion, and dysuria for 3 days. Now hypotensive and minimally responsive. Had urinary catheterization 1 week ago.
patient.history: BPH, type 2 diabetes, recent urinary catheterization

rubric_notes: Classic septic shock: temp 39.6 + SBP 82 + GCS 11 + tachycardic + tachypneic + hypoxic. ALL three sepsis-triad criteria positive. Multiple critical vitals (SBP <90, HR >100 borderline tachy, RR >20 concerning, SpO2 <94 concerning, temp >38.3 fever, GCS <14 altered). Per RUBRIC §3 sepsis with hypotension = ESI 1 mandatory. Sepsis 3-Hour Bundle starts NOW (blood cultures + lactate + IV antibiotics within 1 hour). Vancomycin allergy = critical med-allergy flag.
